introSection: Professor Screamore's SkyWinder was a steel inverted roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Opened on May 20, 1995 as Mind Eraser, it is a standard Suspended Looping Coaster installation from Vekoma. For 2024, the coaster was renamed, refurbished and the trains replaced.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Mind Eraser opened on May 20, 1995.

In 2017, Samsung Gear VR virtual reality headsets were used to offer the Galactic Attack Virtual Reality Coaster.

Mind Eraser was removed from the park's website in 2022, with no comments about it from the park itself.

In 2023, Six Flags announced that Mind Eraser would be repainted and will be given new trains as part of an overhaul of the Coyote Creek themed area which will be replaced with SteamTown. In May 2024, the new name Professor Screamore's SkyWinder was announced.

The ride reopened on June 22, 2024 with SteamTown.

Professor Screamore's SkyWinder closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

On November 27, 2025, the ride's trains were removed from the park. They arrived at Canada's Wonderland the following day, to be used as parts for Flight Deck.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: The ride is painted dark yellow with maroon supports. From 2005 to 2023, the track was yellow and the supports were orange. Prior to the 2005 season, the track was teal and the supports red.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: After the train is locked and checked, it moves out of the station, towards the chain lift hill. Then, when the train crests the lift hill, it makes a rightward U-turn drop. Next, the train goes through a roll over. Then, it makes a slight turn to the left, before a sidewinder. After the sidewinder, the train makes a 270 degree helix to the right, before a double in-line twist. Then, the train makes a U-turn to the right, before a dip. Finally, the train hits the brake run, before the train makes a right turn to the transfer track. The ride ends when the train makes another right turn to the station.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train originally had eight cars, but at some time the park extended it to ten.

fullText:
Professor Screamore's SkyWinder was a steel inverted roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Opened on May 20, 1995 as Mind Eraser, it is a standard Suspended Looping Coaster installation from Vekoma. For 2024, the coaster was renamed, refurbished and the trains replaced.

Mind Eraser opened on May 20, 1995.

In 2017, Samsung Gear VR virtual reality headsets were used to offer the Galactic Attack Virtual Reality Coaster.

Mind Eraser was removed from the park's website in 2022, with no comments about it from the park itself.

In 2023, Six Flags announced that Mind Eraser would be repainted and will be given new trains as part of an overhaul of the Coyote Creek themed area which will be replaced with SteamTown. In May 2024, the new name Professor Screamore's SkyWinder was announced.

The ride reopened on June 22, 2024 with SteamTown.

Professor Screamore's SkyWinder closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

On November 27, 2025, the ride's trains were removed from the park. They arrived at Canada's Wonderland the following day, to be used as parts for Flight Deck.



The ride is painted dark yellow with maroon supports. From 2005 to 2023, the track was yellow and the supports were orange. Prior to the 2005 season, the track was teal and the supports red.

After the train is locked and checked, it moves out of the station, towards the chain lift hill. Then, when the train crests the lift hill, it makes a rightward U-turn drop. Next, the train goes through a roll over. Then, it makes a slight turn to the left, before a sidewinder. After the sidewinder, the train makes a 270 degree helix to the right, before a double in-line twist. Then, the train makes a U-turn to the right, before a dip. Finally, the train hits the brake run, before the train makes a right turn to the transfer track. The ride ends when the train makes another right turn to the station.

The train originally had eight cars, but at some time the park extended it to ten.
